_meta.description: Deterministic Python-powered operator catalog for advanced computation, charting, statistics, and data transformation that exceed native Excel formula capabilities.

usePythonWhen: computation requires multi-step aggregation across sheets
chart output is requested as image (PNG/SVG)
user explicitly requests Python or pandas
pivot table with custom aggregation functions
text normalization requiring regex or NLP
data reshaping (melt, pivot, unpivot, transpose complex)
joining/merging data from multiple sheets by key
useNativeXlsxWhen: simple cell value or formula edits
chart creation from contiguous range (use XLSX_CHART_CREATE instead)

doNotDo: Never create bins that leave some values unassigned
Never use equal-width binning on highly skewed data without warning
Never use more bins than there are distinct values

boundaries.description: Custom bin boundaries for custom_boundaries method

examples_en: Bin the Age column into 5 equal groups
Create quartile bins for the Salary column
Bucket transaction amounts into Low/Medium/High
examples_pt: Dividir a coluna Idade em 5 grupos iguais
Criar bins de quartil para a coluna Salário
Categorizar valores de transação em Baixo/Médio/Alto

doNotDo: Never merge without showing unmatched key count in preview
Never assume key columns have the same dtype — coerce explicitly
Never create a sheet name that already exists without asking

selectColumns.description: Columns to include from right sheet

examples_en: Join the Sales sheet with the Products sheet on Product ID
Merge employee data with department data by Department Code
Combine invoice and payment sheets by Invoice Number
examples_pt: Juntar planilha de Vendas com Produtos pelo ID do Produto
Mesclar dados de funcionários com departamentos pelo Código
Combinar planilhas de fatura e pagamento pelo Número da Fatura

doNotDo: Never invent column names that do not exist in the sheet
Never assume numeric type without checking source column dtype
Never overwrite existing columns without explicit user confirmation

sourceColumns.description: Column names used in the expression

expression.description: Python/pandas expression to compute, e.g. 'col_a * col_b / 100'

outputColumn.description: Name for the new derived column

outputRangeA1.description: A1 range to write results into (auto-detected if omitted)

examples_en: Create a profit margin column from Revenue and Cost
Derive a new column calculating Unit Price times Quantity
Add a column with the percentage change from last month
examples_pt: Criar coluna de margem de lucro a partir de Receita e Custo
Derivar nova coluna calculando Preço Unitário vezes Quantidade
Adicionar coluna com variação percentual do mês anterior

doNotDo: Never evaluate arbitrary Python code — only pandas expressions
Never import external packages beyond pandas/numpy
Never assume column order — always reference by name
examples_en: Apply a complex formula across 10,000 rows
Compute compound interest for each loan row
Classify rows as High/Medium/Low based on multi-column conditions
examples_pt: Aplicar fórmula complexa em 10.000 linhas
Calcular juros compostos para cada linha de empréstimo
Classificar linhas como Alto/Médio/Baixo baseado em múltiplas condições

doNotDo: Never assume column exists without validating against sheet headers
Never silently drop NaN groups — report them in preview
Never output into existing data range without confirmation

rangeA1.description: Source data range (entire used range if omitted)

func.description: sum, mean, median, min, max, count, std, var, first, last

outputSheetName.description: Sheet to write results (new sheet created if does not exist)

outputRangeA1.description: A1 cell for top-left of output

examples_en: Group sales by Region and sum the Revenue column
Aggregate expenses by Department showing sum and average
Summarize orders by product category with count and total
examples_pt: Agrupar vendas por Região e somar a coluna Receita
Agregar despesas por Departamento mostrando soma e média
Resumir pedidos por categoria de produto com contagem e total

doNotDo: Never compute percentile rank on non-numeric columns
Never confuse percentile rank with percentile value
Never rank across groups unless partition is specified

method.description: average, min, max, first, dense (default: average)

examples_en: Add percentile rank for the Sales column
Rank employees by performance score within each department
Compute dense rank of Revenue by Region
examples_pt: Adicionar rank percentual para a coluna Vendas
Classificar funcionários por pontuação de desempenho em cada departamento
Calcular rank denso de Receita por Região

doNotDo: Never apply window function without sorting data by the partition key first
Never assume date column is sorted — always sort explicitly
Never use window size larger than partition group size

column.description: Column to compute window function on

windowFunc.description: rolling_mean, rolling_sum, cumsum, cumprod, rank, pct_change, lag, lead, ewm

Add a cumulative sum column for Sales
Compute month-over-month percentage change
examples_pt: Calcular média móvel de 3 meses da Receita
Adicionar coluna de soma acumulada de Vendas

doNotDo: Never use stacked area when values are not additive
Never use area chart for categorical comparisons
Never omit baseline at zero for area charts
examples_en: Create a stacked area chart of expenses over time
Show cumulative revenue by product line
Plot an area chart of market share trends
examples_pt: Criar gráfico de área empilhada de despesas ao longo do tempo
Mostrar receita acumulada por linha de produto
Plotar gráfico de área de tendências de participação de mercado

doNotDo: Never truncate category labels — rotate or abbreviate if needed
Never use more than 8 colors without a legend
Never stack bars without labeling total values
examples_en: Create a bar chart of Sales by Product
Make a horizontal bar chart ranking departments by expense
Show stacked bar chart of revenue breakdown by quarter

Fazer gráfico de barras horizontal classificando departamentos por despesa
Mostrar gráfico de barras empilhadas da divisão de receita por trimestre

doNotDo: Never suppress outlier points — always show them
Never compare box plots with vastly different scales without dual axes
Never omit median line from box plot

groupBy.description: Column to group by for side-by-side boxes

showOutliers.description: Whether to show outlier points (default: true)

examples_en: Create box plots comparing salary by department
Show box plot of quarterly revenue
Visualize the distribution spread of product prices
examples_pt: Criar box plots comparando salário por departamento
Mostrar box plot de receita trimestral
Visualizar a dispersão de distribuição dos preços dos produtos

doNotDo: Never combine more than 3 chart types in one combo chart
Never use dual axes without clear labeling on both sides
Never mix incompatible chart types (e.g., pie with line)
examples_en: Create a combo chart with bars for Revenue and a line for Growth Rate
Show sales as bars with margin percentage as a line on secondary axis

Mostrar vendas como barras com percentual de margem como linha no eixo secundário

doNotDo: Never create heatmap with >50 rows or columns without warning about readability
Never use rainbow color map — use sequential or diverging scales
Never omit annotations when cell count is under 100

colorMap.description: Color map name: Blues, RdYlGn, coolwarm, viridis (default: coolwarm)

examples_en: Create a heatmap of the correlation matrix
Show a heatmap of monthly sales by region
Visualize employee performance scores as a heatmap
examples_pt: Criar mapa de calor da matriz de correlação
Mostrar mapa de calor de vendas mensais por região

doNotDo: Never choose bin count that hides distribution shape (minimum 10, maximum 100)
Never use histogram for categorical data — use bar chart instead
Never omit Y-axis label (frequency or density)
examples_en: Create a histogram of invoice amounts
Show the distribution of employee salaries
Plot a histogram with density curve for response times
examples_pt: Criar histograma de valores de faturas
Mostrar distribuição de salários dos funcionários
Plotar histograma com curva de densidade para tempos de resposta

doNotDo: Never invent data points — only plot existing data
Never use misleading axis scales (e.g., non-zero baseline for bar charts)
Never omit axis labels or chart title

insertIntoSheet.description: Whether to embed chart image into the workbook

doNotDo: Never create pie chart with more than 8 slices — group small slices as 'Other'
Never use pie chart for time-series data
Never omit percentage labels on slices

maxSlices.description: Max slices before grouping remainder as 'Other' (default: 8)

examples_en: Create a pie chart of market share by company
Show expense distribution as a pie chart
Make a pie chart of revenue by business unit

doNotDo: Never plot scatter on categorical data — use bar chart instead
Never omit trend line if user asks for correlation visualization
Never auto-scale axes to hide outliers without noting them

trendline.description: Whether to add a trend line (default: false)

examples_en: Create a scatter plot of Price vs Quantity
Plot Ad Spend against Conversion Rate with trend line

doNotDo: Never auto-decide which bars are increases vs decreases — derive from data sign
Never omit the total/net bar at the end
Never use waterfall for non-additive data

Show profit & loss breakdown as a waterfall

Mostrar decomposição de lucros e perdas em cascata

doNotDo: Never auto-merge fuzzy duplicates without showing match pairs first
Never use similarity threshold below 0.8 without warning
Never fuzzy match on numeric columns — use exact match instead

column.description: Column to find fuzzy duplicates in

examples_en: Find fuzzy duplicate company names
Flag near-duplicate entries in the Address column
Deduplicate customer names with 85% similarity threshold
examples_pt: Encontrar nomes de empresas duplicados aproximados

doNotDo: Never fill missing values with mean/median without disclosing it
Never drop rows with any missing value by default — always ask which columns matter
Never impute categorical columns with numeric strategies

columns.description: Specific columns to handle (all if omitted)

strategy.description: drop_rows, fill_mean, fill_median, fill_mode, fill_value, fill_forward, fill_backward, interpolate

fillValue.description: Value to use for fill_value strategy

minNonNull.description: Drop columns with fewer than this many non-null values

examples_en: Fill missing values in Revenue with the column median
Drop rows where all key columns are empty
Forward-fill missing dates in the timeline
examples_pt: Preencher valores ausentes em Receita com a mediana da coluna

doNotDo: Never coerce to numeric without handling non-numeric values (use errors='coerce')
Never silently drop unconvertible values — flag them
Never change date format without confirming with user which format is source

dateFormat.description: Date format string for datetime conversion

Parse the Date column as datetime with format DD/MM/YYYY
Coerce the Status column to boolean

doNotDo: Never use exchange rates without stating the rate date
Never assume currency from column values — require explicit source/target currency
Never convert without showing the exchange rate used

fromCurrency.description: ISO 4217 source currency code (e.g., USD, EUR, BRL)

exchangeRate.description: Fixed exchange rate (if not provided, uses column/static rate)

rateDate.description: Date for the exchange rate (ISO format)

doNotDo: Never extract date features from non-date columns without coercion
Never assume date format — detect or ask user
Never create is_weekend feature without checking locale business calendar

features.description: year, month, day, weekday, quarter, week_of_year, day_of_year, is_weekend, is_month_end, is_quarter_end, days_since_epoch

doNotDo: Never one-hot encode columns with >50 unique values without warning about sparsity
Never discard the original column after encoding without confirmation
Never label-encode ordinal data without preserving order

dropFirst.description: Drop first category to avoid multicollinearity (default: false)

doNotDo: Never normalize columns that contain categorical or ID data
Never z-score normalize with zero standard deviation — handle constant columns
Never min-max normalize when outliers dominate the range
examples_en: Normalize the Revenue column to 0-1 range
Apply z-score normalization to all numeric features

doNotDo: Never automatically remove outliers — only flag them
Never use IQR method on non-normal distributions without warning
Never label as outlier without showing the threshold used

threshold.description: IQR multiplier or z-score threshold (default: 1.5 for IQR, 3.0 for z-score)

outputColumn.description: Name for the outlier flag column (default: '_outlier')

Flag anomalous values in transaction amounts
Find statistical outliers across all numeric columns

doNotDo: Never remove outliers without first running PY_OUTLIER_DETECT and showing results
Never remove more than 10% of data without explicit user confirmation
Never remove outliers from financial audit data without strong justification
examples_en: Remove rows with outlier values in the Amount column
Cap outlier values at the IQR bounds
Replace outlier cells with blank values
examples_pt: Remover linhas com valores outlier na coluna Valor

doNotDo: Never create pivot with more than 100 unique row values without pagination warning
Never silently drop null index entries — report them
Never assume aggregation function — ask user or default to sum with disclosure

aggFunc.description: sum, mean, count, min, max, median (default: sum)

doNotDo: Never unpivot ID columns — only value columns
Never lose the original row identifier during melt
Never create output that exceeds 1M rows without warning

doNotDo: Never stack sheets with different column schemas without aligning columns first
Never lose the source sheet identifier — always add a source column
Never silently skip sheets that fail to parse

addSourceColumn.description: Add a column indicating source sheet name (default: true)

sourceColumnName.description: Name for the source indicator column (default: 'Source')

doNotDo: Never transpose a table with mixed header types without warning
Never lose header names during transposition
Never transpose in-place — always write to new range or sheet

hasHeaders.description: Whether first row is header (default: true)

doNotDo: Never sample without setting a random seed for reproducibility
Never stratified sample without verifying stratum column has >1 value per group
Never output sample that is larger than the original dataset

doNotDo: Never compute correlation on non-numeric columns
Never present correlation as causation — add disclaimer
Never include columns with >50% missing values without warning

columns.description: Columns to include in correlation matrix

doNotDo: Never include non-numeric columns in statistical summary without noting they are categorical
Never suppress missing value count — always show it
Never round beyond 4 decimal places for financial data

doNotDo: Never select test type without checking normality assumption
Never report p-value without significance level context
Never claim statistical significance without stating alpha

testType.description: t_test_ind, t_test_paired, chi_square, anova, mann_whitney, wilcoxon

doNotDo: Never run regression on non-numeric columns without coercion warning
Never report R² without also reporting p-values
Never extrapolate beyond data range without explicit disclaimer

doNotDo: Never apply normalization that changes meaning (e.g., normalizing proper nouns to lowercase)
Never strip accents from Portuguese text unless explicitly requested
Never assume encoding — detect and handle UTF-8, Latin-1 gracefully

operations.description: strip, lower, upper, title, remove_punctuation, remove_digits, normalize_unicode, remove_extra_whitespace, remove_html_tags

doNotDo: Never use regex that can cause catastrophic backtracking
Never assume all rows will match — always report match rate
Never discard non-matching rows without user consent

pattern.description: Python regex pattern with capture groups

groupIndex.description: Capture group index to extract (default: 0 = full match)

doNotDo: Never split without handling rows that have fewer delimiters than expected
Never overwrite adjacent columns — always insert new columns
Never assume delimiter without inspecting data first

doNotDo: Never decompose with fewer data points than 2 full seasonal cycles
Never assume additive model — check if multiplicative is more appropriate
Never report trend without noting the decomposition method used

doNotDo: Never forecast without at least 12 data points
Never present forecast without confidence intervals
Never forecast more than 50% of historical data length without warning
Never claim forecast is a prediction — always state it is an estimate

doNotDo: Never write results to a range that contains data without confirmation
Never write more rows or columns than the target range allows
Never write without validating dtypes match Excel-compatible types

outputRangeA1.description: Target A1 range to write results

includeHeaders.description: Write column headers in first row (default: true)

formatNumbers.description: Apply number formatting to numeric cells (default: true)
